compatibleScales.musicalContext: Contains all four chord tones perfectly. The b7 (F) is a chord tone. Essential for V7-I resolution in all styles.
compatibleScales.commonUse: Essential for all V7 situations. Perfect for ii-V-I (Dm7-G7-Cmaj7). Use in jazz, rock, blues, pop, and all dominant harmony.
compatibleScales.famousExamples: Emphasize 3rd (B) and b7 (F) - the tritone that resolves to C. These create dominant character and pull to C major.

compatibleScales.musicalContext: C major over G7 maintains key center. G Mixolydian and C Ionian are the same notes. Simple and effective.
compatibleScales.commonUse: Perfect for maintaining key awareness over V7. Works universally. Great for teaching diatonic harmony.
compatibleScales.famousExamples: Think C major, emphasize G as root. The F creates dominant 7th automatically. Maintains clear tonal direction to C.

compatibleScales.musicalContext: Adds blue notes for authentic blues dominant. The b5 (Db) creates characteristic blues tension. Essential for blues and rock.
compatibleScales.commonUse: Essential for blues and blues-rock G7. Perfect for 12-bar blues. Use when you want gritty, authentic blues dominant.
compatibleScales.famousExamples: Use Db (b5) as passing tone to D. Creates authentic blues sound. The minor 3rd (Bb) adds bluesy tension.

compatibleScales.musicalContext: Contains b9, #9, #11, b13 for maximum tension. Used in jazz for dramatic V7 resolution. Very dissonant, modern sound.
compatibleScales.commonUse: Use for modern jazz G7alt chords. Perfect for dramatic V7-I in bebop, post-bop, and fusion. Very advanced.
compatibleScales.famousExamples: Maximum tension for dramatic jazz resolutions. All alterations create strong pull to C. Very advanced, bebop sound.
